news.summary: In a recent report, it was revealed that over 2,000 new cases of COVID-19 have been confirmed in the past 24 hours, bringing the total number of cases in the country to more than 50,000. Additionally, the number of deaths related to the virus has surpassed 700, with 15 new fatalities reported. These concerning numbers highlight the ongoing impact of the pandemic and the importance of following safety guidelines to prevent further spread.
news.headline: Schumer calls for grounding helicopter company following deadly crash.

news.summary: Today, on April 13, 2025, CNOOC Limited disclosed the commencement of production for the Wenchang 9-7 Oilfield Development Project. The Company, listed as SEHK: 00883 (HKD Counter) and 80883 (RMB Counter), as well as SSE: 600938, revealed the initiation of operations in this project situated in the western Pearl River Mouth Basin.
news.headline: CNOOC begins production at Wenchang 9-7 Oilfield Development Project, located in Pearl River Mouth Basin.

news.summary: New figures have been released regarding the economic growth of the country. The latest data shows a significant increase in the GDP, which rose by 5.2% in the last quarter. Additionally, the unemployment rate has dropped to 3.8%, marking a decrease of 1.2% compared to the previous year. These new statistics indicate a positive trend in the economy and job market, suggesting a stable and growing financial landscape.
news.headline: Senator Schumer calls for FAA to suspend operations of helicopter company following a deadly crash.

news.summary: In a recent development, it has been reported that 3 out of every 5 adults in the country are currently facing financial difficulties, with a staggering 45% unable to afford basic necessities like food and housing. This alarming situation underscores the urgent need for government intervention to address the growing economic crisis. Additionally, it has been found that 1 in every 4 households is living below the poverty line, further emphasizing the severity of the situation. The information reveals a dire need for immediate action to provide relief and support to those struggling with financial hardship.
news.headline: Trump’s tariffs fuel broad inflation concerns, CBS Poll reveals.
Apple secures temporary exemption from tariffs; relief for now.
NZ March services PSI rises to 49.1, BNZ
Invitation for loan debentures issuance showcased by Finance Innovations, seeking Expressions of Interest.

news.summary: Norsk Hydro ASA has recently completed the issuance of EUR 500 million in senior unsecured green bonds as part of its Euro Medium Term Note (EMTN) Programme. These newly issued bonds feature a 7-year tenor and an annual fixed coupon rate of 3.625 percent (3.649 percent reoffer yield).
news.headline: Norsk Hydro placed 500 million EUR green bonds with 7-year tenor at fixed 3.625% coupon rate.

news.summary: Grupo Aeroportuario del Pacífico, S.A.B. de C.V. announced the successful completion of issuing 60 million long-term bond certificates in Mexico for a total of Ps. 6.0 billion. The issuance was split into two tranches, with the initial amount set at Ps. 5.0 billion and a greenshoe option of up to 20% which was fully exercised. The order book exceeded expectations with a 3.4x oversubscription over the initial amount of Ps. 5.0 billion.
news.headline: Grupo Aeroportuario del Pacifico issued Ps. 6.0 billion in bond certificates in Mexico with a subscription of 3.4x over Ps. 5.0 billion.

news.summary: In Sarasota, Florida on April 11, 2025, Trump Media and Technology Group Corp. (TMTG) disclosed that its registration statement on Form S-3 (No. 333-286327) submitted to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) on April 1, 2025, is now in effect. TMTG, listed on Nasdaq and NYSE Texas as DJT, operates Truth Social, Truth+ streaming platform, and Truth.Fi FinTech brand. The SEC confirmed non-review of the filing to the company.
news.headline: Trump Media and Technology Group Corp.'s Form S-3 (No. 333-286327) becomes effective after the SEC's review.

news.summary: Teniz Capital Investment Banking will lead the second phase of bond placement for BSTDB on the Astana International Exchange, following a first tranche of 100 million USD completed in 2024. The second tranche aims to support BSTDB's funding capacity and enhance investor participation in Central Asian markets. BSTDB, founded in 1999 in Greece, has 11 member states. Teniz Capital, with 50 professionals, completed 13 bond transactions in 2023 and has headquarters in Almaty, with offices in Astana and Abu Dhabi. On 29 August last year, Teniz Capital announced the launch of a sister company, Teniz Capital Brokerage Ltd.
news.headline: Teniz Capital to lead second phase of BSTDB bond placement on AIX after first tranche of 100 million USD in 2024.

news.summary: In recent developments, it has been reported that the unemployment rate has dropped to 4.2%, representing a decrease of 0.5% compared to last month. The economy added 250,000 jobs in the same period, exceeding expectations and marking a significant improvement in the labor market. This positive trend is attributed to various factors, including increased consumer spending and business investments. Additionally, the average hourly earnings saw a slight increase of 2.5%, indicating potential growth in personal income. These latest figures suggest a strengthening economy and provide hope for continued job market stability in the near future.
news.headline: $450M Green Bond Offering by Allied announced.

news.summary: Fairfax Financial Holdings Limited (TSX: FFH and FFH.U) has disclosed the outcome of the Director vote at its Annual Shareholders’ Meeting held on April 10, 2025, in Toronto. The company expressed contentment with the results of the vote.
news.headline: Fairfax Financial Holdings Limited announced results of voting for Directors at April 10, 2025 Annual Shareholders’ Meeting.

news.summary: Sweden is required to contribute to the EU's budget on a monthly basis as part of its membership. The Swedish government makes these payments in Swedish kronor, which are then exchanged into euros by a central bank in the EU. This exchange involves significant amounts of money each month, reflecting Sweden's financial commitment to the EU.
news.headline: Sweden makes monthly euro payments to EU, converted from Swedish Kronor by recipient central bank within EU.

news.summary: Mink Ventures has completed the first tranche of its CMETC eligible flow-through and hard dollar financing. The company secured a significant amount of funds in this initial closing. The second and final close of this financing is scheduled for April 25th. This financing round is crucial for the company's ongoing operations and growth strategy.
news.headline: Mink Ventures closes 1st tranche of financing for critical minerals; 2nd close on April 25.

news.summary: In the latest update, it has been reported that production at a major manufacturing plant has been disrupted due to a cyberattack. The attack has resulted in a loss of approximately 500 units of product, which is estimated to be worth around $1 million. The company is currently working to restore operations and enhance its cybersecurity measures to prevent future incidents. The attack has raised concerns about the vulnerability of industrial systems to cyber threats.
news.headline: Allied wraps up $450M eco-friendly bond sale.

news.summary: Toronto, April 11, 2025 - Northland Power Inc. disclosed that John Brace will step down as Chair of the Board after the upcoming Annual General Meeting on May 21, 2025. If elected, Brace will continue as a director. Ian Pearce, the current Lead Independent Director and Chair of the Governance and Nominating Committee, is set to assume the role of Chair of the Board following the AGM. Northland Power Inc. is listed as TSX: NPI.
news.headline: Northland Power plans board chair succession: John Brace transitions, Ian Pearce to take over at AGM on May 21, 2025.

news.summary: This information is intended for international distribution only and should not be released to U.S. newswire services or disseminated within the United States.
news.headline: Teranet will redeem 3.544% Series 2020-1 senior secured bonds after closing $550M senior secured bond offering.

news.summary: On April 11, 2025, First Financial Northwest, Inc. disclosed the completion of a transaction where Global Federal Credit Union obtained most assets and liabilities of First Financial Northwest Bank. The deal, finalized on the same day, resulted in the Company receiving $228.7 million in cash as per the Purchase and Assumption Agreement from January 10, 2024.
news.headline: First Financial Northwest, Inc. sold First Financial Northwest Bank to Global Federal Credit Union for $228.7 million in cash.

news.summary: Casella Waste Systems, Inc. has announced an offering of up to $25.0 million aggregate principal amount of Vermont Economic Development Authority Solid Waste Disposal Revenue Bonds Series 2022A-2. This is a drawdown of the remainder of a previous loan taken in June 2022 worth $35.0 million. The new Bonds will be guaranteed by Casella's subsidiaries and will be used to finance capital projects in the State of Vermont. The offering must occur by June 1, 2025, after which the funds will no longer be available.
news.headline: Casella Waste Systems, Inc. to offer up to $25M in Vermont Economic Development Authority Solid Waste Disposal Revenue Bonds.

news.summary: On April 11, 2025, Jo-Jo Capital Canada Ltd. announced the resignation of Shant Poladian as a director, effective April 30, 2025.
news.headline: JS Capital Canada Ltd. announces Shant Poladian resigns as director; effective April 30, 2025.
Beijing faces deflation as prices drop, posing challenges for China’s economy in the future.
Key points to watch for in Trump's Congress address: 7 important things to keep an eye on.

news.summary: On March 17, 2025, RBAZ Bancorp, Inc. (OTCPK: RBAZ) shared that it has received all necessary regulatory approvals for the sale of the majority of assets and liabilities of Republic Bank of Arizona to Pima Federal Credit Union. This sale is being executed under the Purchase and Assumption Agreement dated May 16, 2024. Shareholders of RBAZ have also given their approval for this sale. The Asset Sale is set to be finalized on May 2, 2025, pending the fulfillment of standard closing conditions outlined in the Purchase Agreement.
news.headline: RBAZ Bancorp, Inc. to close Asset Sale to Pima Federal Credit Union on May 2, 2025 after regulatory approval.

news.summary: On April 11, 2025, Partners Value Split Corp. (TSX: PVS.PR.G, PVS.PR.H, PVS.PR.I, PVS.PR.J, PVS.PR.K, PVS.PR.L) appointed Cyrus Madon as the new Chief Executive Officer. Additionally, the company welcomed Aleks Novakovic, Paul Farrell, and Don Mackenzie as new members of the board of directors, replacing outgoing members Frank Lochan, Gregory Morrison, and Ralph J. Zarboni who are retiring.
news.headline: Partners Value Split Corp. appoints Cyrus Madon as new CEO and welcomes three new board members, replacing three retiring members.

news.summary: The Children's Place, Inc. reported its financial results for the fourth quarter ended February 1, 2025, including: net sales of $409 million for the quarter and $1.386 billion for the full year, a significant improvement in gross profit margin reaching 29% for the quarter and 33% for the year, and a decrease in net sales due to factors like decreased e-commerce revenue. The company experienced an operating income improvement of $68.6 million for the quarter compared to the previous year. The completion of a $90 million rights offering post year-end positively impacted the liquidity position. The number of stores decreased to 495 by the end of the year, and the company emphasized efforts on refining omni-channel strategy and product mix for the upcoming fiscal year. The balance sheet showed $5.3 million in cash, $40.2 million borrowing availability, and total liquidity of $85.5 million as of February 1, 2025. Inventory was reported at $399.6 million on the same date. The company had an operating loss of $(13.7) million for the fiscal year but adjusted operating income of $52.7 million, showing an improvement from the previous year. Net loss was $(57.8) million for the year, including certain non-cash items. Adjusted net income was $5.5 million for the year. It opened a Gymboree store during the fourth quarter, closed 16 stores, and ended the year with 495 stores. The company is focusing on enhancing its store portfolio and executing strategic initiatives to improve profitability and business performance.
news.headline: The Children's Place reports fourth quarter and full year 2024 results, with net sales of $409 million for Q4 and $1.386 billion for the year, a significant improvement in gross profit margin to 29% for Q4 and 33% for the year. Adjusted operating income reached $68.6 million for Q4. Lowest SG&A spending in over 15 years for Q4 and the year. Raised $90 million in capital through a rights offering. Operating loss was $(13.7) million for the year. The Company ended the year with 495 stores. Total liquidity was $85.5 million as of February 1, 2025. Net loss for the year was $(57.8) million. Adjusted net income reached $5.5 million.

news.summary: Lina Khan, along with her supporters, sought to reform antitrust regulations with a view to reshaping the field. The administration led by Trump seems inclined to halt these efforts.
news.headline: Lina Khan and her allies' antitrust law reform may end as Trump's team steps in.

news.summary: Casella Waste Systems, Inc. disclosed today that it has set the price for an offering of up to $25.0 million aggregate principal amount of Vermont Economic Development Authority Solid Waste Disposal Revenue Bonds. These bonds, known as Casella Waste Systems, Inc. Project Series 2022A-2, will be issued under an indenture between the Issuer and the bond trustee dated June 1, 2022. This offering represents the drawdown of the remaining funds from the Vermont Economic Development Authority Solid Waste Disposal Revenue Bonds (Casella Waste Systems, Inc. Project) Series 2022, with an initial loan of $35.0 million provided to the Company back in June 2022. If this drawdown does not occur by June 1, 2025, the funds would no longer be accessible to the Company.
news.headline: Casella Waste Systems prices $25.0 million of Vermont Economic Development Authority Solid Waste Disposal Revenue Bonds.

news.summary: Lufkin has recently sold its Downhole business in North America to Q2 Artificial Lift Services. Despite this sale, Lufkin will continue to operate its Downhole business internationally. This move entails a transfer of key assets, including equipment and employees, to Q2 Artificial Lift Services. The sale is estimated to amount to approximately $X million.
news.headline: Lufkin Industries sells NA Downhole business to Q2 Artificial Lift Services, retains international business.

news.summary: In the latest statistics released, it has been reported that the number of confirmed COVID-19 cases has reached over 5.6 million globally, with the death toll exceeding 350,000. This data underscores the continued impact and severity of the pandemic across numerous countries. Additionally, healthcare professionals have highlighted the importance of adhering to safety guidelines, such as wearing masks and practicing social distancing, to mitigate the spread of the virus. The World Health Organization continues to monitor the situation and provide guidance to nations as they navigate this public health crisis.
news.headline: Lithium company extends closing of private finance placement, delaying completion by several weeks.
Wolters Kluwer sets price for €500M seven-year Eurobond issuance.

news.summary: The Treasury secretary is not likely to ease Trump's tough trade policies, dashing Wall Street investors' expectations regarding Scott Bessent's possible influence.
news.headline: Investors disappointed as Scott Bessent doesn't moderate trade policies like Steven Mnuchin as Treasury secretary.

news.summary: On March 19, 2025, in Elkhart, Indiana, THOR Industries, Inc. revealed a significant reorganization plan to enhance its position as the top player in the recreational vehicle (RV) sector. The strategic restructuring targeted at improving the overall enterprise structure and solidifying the brand portfolio includes integrating Heartland Recreational Vehicles into Jayco, Inc. This move is designed to boost operational effectiveness and simplify business procedures within the company. THOR Industries, Inc., listed on the New York Stock Exchange under the symbol THO, is a key player in the RV industry.
news.headline: THOR Industries restructures: Heartland RV integrated under Jayco to enhance efficiency.

news.summary: The Federal Reserve officials are set to release their quarterly economic forecast on Wednesday afternoon. This forecast is highly anticipated and will clash with President Donald Trump's agenda.
news.headline: Federal Reserve is expected to release quarterly forecast colliding with President Trump's agenda.

news.summary: The implementation of new tariffs by President Trump, amidst already existing policy uncertainty, is expected to negatively impact economic growth. The crucial concern lies in determining the magnitude of the impact it will have on the economy.
news.headline: Trump's new tariffs amid policy uncertainty will impact growth; extent of hit remains uncertain.

news.summary: In the latest report, it was revealed that over 10,000 new cases of COVID-19 have been recorded in the last 24 hours in the country. This marks the highest single-day increase since the pandemic began, bringing the total number of confirmed cases to more than 150,000. Additionally, the number of deaths has also risen significantly, with 250 new fatalities reported. Health officials are urging the public to adhere to safety measures such as wearing masks and practicing social distancing to curb the spread of the virus. The government is considering implementing stricter restrictions to contain the escalating situation.
news.headline: Trump's tariffs linked to broad inflation fears, according to latest CBS poll data.

news.summary: Trump was reluctant to make predictions about the possibility of a recession when questioned during a recorded interview with Maria Bartiromo on "Sunday Morning Futures."
news.headline: Trump does not rule out recession in 2025, hesitates to predict it in a recorded interview with Maria Bartiromo.

news.summary: On March 21, 2025, a prominent Turkish e-commerce platform, D-MARKET Electronic Services & Trading, also known as “Hepsiburada,” revealed through GLOBE NEWSWIRE that its indirect wholly owned subsidiary, Hepsi Finansman A.Ş. (“Hepsifinans”), has initiated its third bond issuance. This issuance amounts to a significant nominal value of TRY 100 million. The company, listed on NASDAQ under the symbol HEPS, continues to make strides in the e-commerce sector through such financial ventures.
news.headline: Hepsiburada's subsidiary, Hepsifinans, announces third bond issuance worth TRY 100 million on March 21, 2025.

news.summary: In Vancouver, British Columbia, on April 1, 2025, Patagonia Gold Corp. disclosed that they have handed back three out of the fourteen properties belonging to the Nico project to Mirasol Resources Ltd. The company, also known as Patagonia, (TSXV:PGDC) has decided to give up the other eleven properties as well.
news.headline: Patagonia Gold Corp. returns 3 of 14 Nico properties to Mirasol Resources Ltd., relinquishing 11 others.

news.summary: On April 11, 2025, TOMI Environmental Solutions, Inc.® (NASDAQ: TOMZ), based in Frederick, Md., stated that it will release its fourth-quarter results for the period ending on December 31, 2024, after the financial markets close on Monday, April 14, 2025. Following this release, the company plans to conduct a conference call on the same day at 4:30 p.m. Eastern Time to discuss the results. TOMI is known as a global specialist in offering disinfection and decontamination solutions. Stay tuned for further details on the financial performance of TOMI.
news.headline: TOMI Environmental Solutions to discuss Q4 2024 financial results on April 14, 2025, through a conference call at 4:30 p.m. ET.
CFOs facing recession amid tariff turmoil.
